title: Ecosystem
subtitle: Ecosystem structure, productivity, decomposition, energy flow, ecological pyramids, nutrient cycles and succession - a visual ecology dashboard.

heroModel.title: Energy flow ecosystem
heroModel.subtitle: Drag to rotate - scroll to zoom

roadmapTitle: Ecosystem is energy flow plus nutrient cycling.
roadmapDescription: Learn structure first, then productivity, decomposition, trophic transfer, pyramids, cycles, succession and services.
flowchart:
flowchart LR
  A[Ecosystem structure] --> B[Productivity]
  B --> C[Decomposition]
  C --> D[Energy flow]
  D --> E[Food chain and web]
  E --> F[Ecological pyramids]
  F --> G[Nutrient cycling]
  G --> H[Succession]
  H --> I[Ecosystem services]

roadmap.title: Ecosystem structure
roadmap.summary: Abiotic components plus producers, consumers and decomposers.

roadmap.title: Productivity
roadmap.summary: Rate of biomass production as GPP and NPP.

roadmap.title: Decomposition
roadmap.summary: Detritus is broken down through fragmentation, leaching, catabolism, humification and mineralisation.

roadmap.title: Energy flow
roadmap.summary: Energy moves one way through trophic levels.

roadmap.title: Pyramids
roadmap.summary: Number, biomass and energy pyramids compare trophic levels.

roadmap.title: Nutrient cycles
roadmap.summary: Carbon and phosphorus cycle through biotic and abiotic pools.

roadmap.title: Succession
roadmap.summary: Communities change from pioneer stage to climax.

conceptTitle: Ecosystem as a moving flow diagram.
conceptDescription: Every concept becomes a transfer: sunlight to biomass, detritus to minerals, nutrients to organisms and back.

sections.title: Ecosystem components
sections.description: Ecosystems include abiotic factors and interacting living organisms.
points: Producers convert solar energy into biomass.
Consumers depend directly or indirectly on producers.
Decomposers break down dead organic matter.
Abiotic pool includes nutrients, water, temperature and light.
sections.neet: Producers form the base of most ecosystems.

sections.title: GPP and NPP
sections.description: Productivity is the rate of biomass production.
points: GPP is total organic matter produced by photosynthesis.
Respiration consumes part of GPP.
NPP equals GPP minus respiration.
NPP is available to consumers.
sections.neet: NPP = GPP - R is a high-yield formula.

sections.title: Decomposition steps
sections.description: Decomposition converts detritus into simpler substances and minerals.
points: Fragmentation breaks detritus into smaller particles.
Leaching removes soluble nutrients.
Catabolism enzymatically degrades detritus.
Humification forms humus and mineralisation releases inorganic nutrients.
sections.neet: Warm, moist and aerobic conditions favour decomposition.

sections.title: Energy flow and trophic levels
sections.description: Energy flow through ecosystem is unidirectional.
points: Sun is main energy source.
Only a fraction transfers to next trophic level.
Food chains link producers, consumers and decomposers.
Food webs provide stability.
sections.neet: Energy is not recycled; nutrients are recycled.

sections.title: Ecological pyramids
sections.description: Pyramids represent trophic levels in number, biomass or energy.
points: Energy pyramid is always upright.
Pyramid of biomass may be inverted in aquatic systems.
Pyramid of number may be inverted in tree ecosystem.
Each trophic level loses energy as heat.

sections.title: Nutrient cycling
sections.description: Nutrients cycle between organisms and abiotic pools.
points: Carbon cycle includes photosynthesis, respiration, decomposition and combustion.
Phosphorus cycle lacks a major gaseous phase.
Reservoirs store nutrients.
Decomposers are essential for recycling.
sections.neet: Phosphorus cycle is sedimentary and has no significant atmospheric phase.

sections.title: Ecological succession
sections.description: Communities change gradually toward a relatively stable climax community.
points: Primary succession begins on bare areas.
Secondary succession begins where soil already exists.
Pioneer species start succession.
Xerarch succession begins in dry areas, hydrarch in wet areas.
sections.neet: Secondary succession is faster than primary succession.

sections.title: Ecosystem services
sections.description: Ecosystems provide benefits that support human life.
points: Pollination, climate regulation and oxygen production are services.
Forests reduce erosion and regulate water cycles.
Biodiversity supports stability.
Healthy ecosystems have economic and survival value.
sections.neet: Ecosystem services are natural life-support services.

modelDescription: Rotate energy flow, pyramids and nutrient cycle schematics.
highYield: Decomposition steps include fragmentation, leaching, catabolism, humification and mineralisation.
Warm moist aerobic conditions favour decomposition.
Biomass pyramid can be inverted in aquatic ecosystem.
Number pyramid can be inverted in tree ecosystem.
Phosphorus has no major gaseous phase.
Primary succession starts on bare area.

mistakes.title: Energy vs nutrients
mistakes.body: Energy flows one way; nutrients cycle.

mistakes.title: GPP vs NPP
mistakes.body: NPP is not total production. It is GPP minus respiration.

mistakes.title: Always upright?
mistakes.body: Only energy pyramid is always upright; number and biomass pyramids can be inverted.

quiz.question: NPP equals:
quiz.explain: Net primary productivity is GPP minus respiratory loss.

quiz.question: Energy flow in ecosystem is:
quiz.explain: Energy enters and is lost as heat; it is not recycled.

quiz.question: Decomposition is fastest in:
quiz.explain: Moisture, warmth and oxygen support decomposers.

quiz.question: Primary succession begins on:
quiz.explain: Primary succession begins where no soil/community exists.

quiz.question: Nutrients in ecosystem:
quiz.explain: Nutrients recycle between biotic and abiotic pools.
